verses.kjv: THE beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
verses.bsb: This is the beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

verses.kjv: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
verses.bsb: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet:  “Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way.”

verses.kjv: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
verses.bsb: “A voice of one calling in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way for the Lord, make straight paths for Him.’”

verses.kjv: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.
verses.bsb: John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.

verses.kjv: And there went out unto him all the land of Judæa, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.
verses.bsb: People went out to him from all of Jerusalem and the countryside of Judea. Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.

verses.kjv: And John was clothed with camel’s hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;
verses.bsb: John was clothed in camel’s hair, with a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.

verses.kjv: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
verses.bsb: And he began to proclaim: “After me will come One more powerful than I, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie.

verses.kjv: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: I baptize you with water, but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.
verses.bsb: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.

verses.kjv: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:
verses.bsb: As soon as Jesus came up out of the water, He saw the heavens breaking open and the Spirit descending on Him like a dove.

verses.kjv: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.
verses.bsb: And a voice came from heaven: “You are My beloved Son; in You I am well pleased.”

verses.kjv: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.
verses.bsb: At once the Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness,

verses.kjv: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
verses.bsb: and He was there for forty days, being tempted by Satan. He was with the wild animals, and the angels ministered to Him.

verses.kjv: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
verses.bsb: After the arrest of John, Jesus went into Galilee and proclaimed the gospel of God.

verses.kjv: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.
verses.bsb: “The time is fulfilled,” He said, “and the kingdom of God is near. Repent and believe in the gospel!”

verses.kjv: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
verses.bsb: As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the sea, for they were fishermen.

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.
verses.bsb: “Come, follow Me,” Jesus said, “and I will make you fishers of men.”

verses.kjv: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
verses.bsb: And at once they left their nets and followed Him.

verses.kjv: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
verses.bsb: Going on a little farther, He saw James son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat, mending their nets.

verses.kjv: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
verses.bsb: Immediately Jesus called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired men and followed Him.

verses.kjv: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus and His companions went to Capernaum, and right away Jesus entered the synagogue on the Sabbath and began to teach.

verses.kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.bsb: The people were astonished at His teaching, because He taught as one who had authority, and not as the scribes.

verses.kjv: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,
verses.bsb: Suddenly a man with an unclean spirit cried out in the synagogue:

verses.kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.
verses.bsb: “What do You want with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!”

verses.kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
verses.bsb: But Jesus rebuked the spirit. “Be silent!” He said. “Come out of him!”

verses.kjv: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.
verses.bsb: At this, the unclean spirit threw the man into convulsions and came out with a loud shriek.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
verses.bsb: All the people were amazed and began to ask one another, “What is this? A new teaching with authority! He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him!”

verses.kjv: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
verses.bsb: And the news about Jesus spread quickly through the whole region of Galilee.

verses.kjv: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
verses.bsb: As soon as Jesus and His companions had left the synagogue, they went with James and John to the home of Simon and Andrew.

verses.kjv: But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.
verses.bsb: Simon’s mother-in-law was sick in bed with a fever, and they promptly told Jesus about her.

verses.kjv: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
verses.bsb: So He went to her, took her by the hand, and helped her up. The fever left her, and she began to serve them.

verses.kjv: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
verses.bsb: That evening, after sunset, people brought to Jesus all who were sick and demon-possessed,

verses.kjv: And all the city was gathered together at the door.
verses.bsb: and the whole town gathered at the door.

verses.kjv: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
verses.bsb: And He healed many who were ill with various diseases and drove out many demons. But He would not allow the demons to speak, because they knew who He was.

verses.kjv: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
verses.bsb: Early in the morning, while it was still dark, Jesus got up and slipped out to a solitary place to pray.

verses.kjv: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
verses.bsb: Simon and his companions went to look for Him,

verses.kjv: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
verses.bsb: and when they found Him, they said, “Everyone is looking for You!”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered, “Let us go on to the neighboring towns so I can preach there as well, for that is why I have come.”

verses.kjv: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.
verses.bsb: So He went throughout Galilee, preaching in their synagogues and driving out demons.

verses.kjv: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
verses.bsb: Then a leper came to Jesus, begging on his knees: “If You are willing, You can make me clean.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
verses.bsb: Moved with compassion, Jesus reached out His hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” He said. “Be clean!”

verses.kjv: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
verses.bsb: And immediately the leprosy left him, and the man was cleansed.

verses.kjv: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
verses.bsb: Jesus promptly sent him away with a stern warning:

verses.kjv: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
verses.bsb: “See that you don’t tell anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and present the offering Moses prescribed for your cleansing, as a testimony to them.”

verses.kjv: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.
verses.bsb: But the man went out and openly began to proclaim and spread the news.  Consequently, Jesus could no longer enter a town in plain view, but He stayed out in solitary places. Yet people came to Him from every quarter.

verses.kjv: AND again he entered into Capernaum, after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.
verses.bsb: A few days later Jesus went back to Capernaum. And when the people heard that He was home,

verses.kjv: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.
verses.bsb: they gathered in such large numbers that there was no more room, not even outside the door, as Jesus spoke the word to them.

verses.kjv: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.
verses.bsb: Then a paralytic was brought to Him, carried by four men.

verses.kjv: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
verses.bsb: Since they were unable to get to Jesus through the crowd, they uncovered the roof above Him, made an opening, and lowered the paralytic on his mat.

verses.kjv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, “Son, your sins are forgiven.”

verses.kjv: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
verses.bsb: But some of the scribes were sitting there and thinking in their hearts,

verses.kjv: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
verses.bsb: “Why does this man speak like this? He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

verses.kjv: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
verses.bsb: At once Jesus knew in His spirit that they were thinking this way within themselves. “Why are you thinking these things in your hearts?” He asked.

verses.kjv: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
verses.bsb: “Which is easier: to say to a paralytic, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, pick up your mat, and walk’?

verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
verses.bsb: But so that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins...” He said to the paralytic,

verses.kjv: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.
verses.bsb: “I tell you, get up, pick up your mat, and go home.”

verses.kjv: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
verses.bsb: And immediately the man got up, picked up his mat, and walked out in front of them all. As a result, they were all astounded and glorified God, saying, “We have never seen anything like this!”

verses.kjv: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus went out beside the sea. All the people came to Him, and He taught them there.

verses.kjv: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphæus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
verses.bsb: As He was walking along, He saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth. “Follow Me,” He told him, and Levi got up and followed Him.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was dining at Levi’s house, many tax collectors and sinners were eating with Him and His disciples—for there were many who followed Him.

verses.kjv: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
verses.bsb: When the scribes who were Pharisees saw Jesus eating with these people, they asked His disciples, “Why does He eat with tax collectors and sinners?”

verses.kjv: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
verses.bsb: On hearing this, Jesus told them, “It is not the healthy who need a doctor, but the sick. I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners.”

verses.kjv: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
verses.bsb: Now John’s disciples and the Pharisees were often fasting. So people came to Jesus and asked, “Why don’t Your disciples fast like John’s disciples and those of the Pharisees?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “How can the guests of the bridegroom fast while He is with them? As long as He is with them, they cannot fast.

verses.kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
verses.bsb: But the time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.

verses.kjv: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
verses.bsb: No one sews a patch of unshrunk cloth on an old garment. If he does, the new piece will pull away from the old, and a worse tear will result.

verses.kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
verses.bsb: And no one pours new wine into old wineskins. If he does, the wine will burst the skins, and both the wine and the wineskins will be ruined. Instead, new wine is poured into new wineskins.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.
verses.bsb: One Sabbath Jesus was passing through the grainfields, and His disciples began to pick the heads of grain as they walked along.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
verses.bsb: So the Pharisees said to Him, “Look, why are they doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath?”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Have you never read what David did when he and his companions were hungry and in need?

verses.kjv: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
verses.bsb: During the high priesthood of Abiathar, he entered the house of God and ate the consecrated bread, which was lawful only for the priests. And he gave some to his companions as well.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
verses.bsb: Then Jesus declared, “The Sabbath was made for man, not man for the Sabbath.

verses.kjv: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.
verses.bsb: Therefore, the Son of Man is Lord even of the Sabbath.”

verses.kjv: AND he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus entered the synagogue, and a man with a withered hand was there.

verses.kjv: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
verses.bsb: In order to accuse Jesus, they were watching to see if He would heal on the Sabbath.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to the man with the withered hand, “Stand up among us.”

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.
verses.bsb: And He asked them, “Which is lawful on the Sabbath: to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy it?”  But they were silent.

verses.kjv: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
verses.bsb: Jesus looked around at them with anger and sorrow at their hardness of heart. Then He said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
verses.bsb: At this, the Pharisees went out and began plotting with the Herodians how they might kill Jesus.

verses.kjv: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judæa,
verses.bsb: So Jesus withdrew with His disciples to the sea, accompanied by a large crowd from Galilee, Judea,

verses.kjv: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumæa, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
verses.bsb: Jerusalem, Idumea, the region beyond the Jordan, and the vicinity of Tyre and Sidon. The large crowd came to Him when they heard what great things He was doing.

verses.kjv: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
verses.bsb: Jesus asked His disciples to have a boat ready for Him so that the crowd would not crush Him.

verses.kjv: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
verses.bsb: For He had healed so many that all who had diseases were pressing forward to touch Him.

verses.kjv: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
verses.bsb: And when the unclean spirits saw Him, they fell down before Him and cried out, “You are the Son of God!”

verses.kjv: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
verses.bsb: But He warned them sternly not to make Him known.

verses.kjv: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus went up on the mountain and called for those He wanted, and they came to Him.

verses.kjv: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,
verses.bsb: He appointed twelve of them, whom He designated as apostles, to accompany Him, to be sent out to preach,

verses.kjv: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:
verses.bsb: and to have authority to drive out demons.

verses.kjv: And Simon he surnamed Peter;
verses.bsb: These are the twelve He appointed: Simon (whom He named Peter),

verses.kjv: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:
verses.bsb: James son of Zebedee and his brother John (whom He named Boanerges, meaning “Sons of Thunder”),

verses.kjv: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphæus, and Thaddæus, and Simon the Canaanite,
verses.bsb: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot,

verses.kjv: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.
verses.bsb: and Judas Iscariot, who betrayed Jesus.

verses.kjv: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus went home, and once again a crowd gathered, so that He and His disciples could not even eat.

verses.kjv: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.
verses.bsb: When His family heard about this, they went out to take custody of Him, saying, “He is out of His mind.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
verses.bsb: And the scribes who had come down from Jerusalem were saying, “He is possessed by Beelzebul,” and, “By the prince of the demons He drives out demons.”

verses.kjv: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
verses.bsb: So Jesus called them together and began to speak to them in parables: “How can Satan drive out Satan?

verses.kjv: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
verses.bsb: If a kingdom is divided against itself, it cannot stand.

verses.kjv: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
verses.bsb: If a house is divided against itself, it cannot stand.

verses.kjv: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
verses.bsb: And if Satan is divided and rises against himself, he cannot stand; his end has come.

verses.kjv: No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
verses.bsb: Indeed, no one can enter a strong man’s house to steal his possessions unless he first ties up the strong man. Then he can plunder his house.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, the sons of men will be forgiven all sins and blasphemies, as many as they utter.

verses.kjv: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:
verses.bsb: But whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven; he is guilty of eternal sin.”

verses.kjv: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
verses.bsb: Jesus made this statement because they were saying, “He has an unclean spirit.”

verses.kjv: ¶ There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers came and stood outside. They sent someone in to summon Him,

verses.kjv: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
verses.bsb: and a crowd was sitting around Him. “Look,” He was told, “Your mother and brothers are outside, asking for You.”

verses.kjv: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
verses.bsb: But Jesus replied, “Who are My mother and My brothers?”

verses.kjv: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
verses.bsb: Looking at those seated in a circle around Him, He said, “Here are My mother and My brothers!

verses.kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.
verses.bsb: For whoever does the will of God is My brother and sister and mother.”

verses.kjv: AND he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus began to teach beside the sea, and such a large crowd gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat in it, while all the people crowded along the shore.

verses.kjv: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,
verses.bsb: And He taught them many things in parables, and in His teaching He said,

verses.kjv: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:
verses.bsb: “Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
verses.bsb: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.

verses.kjv: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
verses.bsb: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.

verses.kjv: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
verses.bsb: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.

verses.kjv: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
verses.bsb: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings, and they yielded no crop.

verses.kjv: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
verses.bsb: Still other seed fell on good soil, where it sprouted, grew up, and produced a crop—one bearing thirtyfold, another sixtyfold, and another a hundredfold.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

verses.kjv: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.
verses.bsb: As soon as Jesus was alone with the Twelve and those around Him, they asked Him about the parable.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:
verses.bsb: He replied, “The mystery of the kingdom of God has been given to you, but to those on the outside everything is expressed in parables,

verses.kjv: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.
verses.bsb: so that,  ‘they may be ever seeing but never perceiving, and ever hearing but never understanding; otherwise they might turn and be forgiven.’”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? Then how will you understand any of the parables?

verses.kjv: ¶ The sower soweth the word.
verses.bsb: The farmer sows the word.

verses.kjv: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
verses.bsb: Some are like the seeds along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.

verses.kjv: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
verses.bsb: Some are like the seeds sown on rocky ground. They hear the word and at once receive it with joy.

verses.kjv: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word’s sake, immediately they are offended.
verses.bsb: But they themselves have no root, and they remain for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.

verses.kjv: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
verses.bsb: Others are like the seeds sown among the thorns. They hear the word,

verses.kjv: And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
verses.bsb: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth, and the desire for other things come in and choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

verses.kjv: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.
verses.bsb: Still others are like the seeds sown on good soil. They hear the word, receive it, and produce a crop—thirtyfold, sixtyfold, or a hundredfold.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
verses.bsb: Jesus also said to them, “Does anyone bring in a lamp to put it under a basket or under a bed? Doesn’t he set it on a stand?

verses.kjv: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
verses.bsb: For there is nothing hidden that will not be disclosed, and nothing concealed that will not be brought to light.

verses.kjv: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
verses.bsb: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.
verses.bsb: He went on to say, “Pay attention to what you hear. With the measure you use, it will be measured to you, and even more will be added to you.

verses.kjv: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.
verses.bsb: For whoever has will be given more. But whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;
verses.bsb: Jesus also said, “The kingdom of God is like a man who scatters seed on the ground.

verses.kjv: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.
verses.bsb: Night and day he sleeps and wakes, and the seed sprouts and grows, though he knows not how.

verses.kjv: For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
verses.bsb: All by itself the earth produces a crop—first the stalk, then the head, then grain that ripens within.

verses.kjv: But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.
verses.bsb: And as soon as the grain is ripe, he swings the sickle, because the harvest has come.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
verses.bsb: Then He asked, “To what can we compare the kingdom of God? With what parable shall we present it?

verses.kjv: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
verses.bsb: It is like a mustard seed, which is the smallest of all seeds sown upon the earth.

verses.kjv: But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.
verses.bsb: But after it is planted, it grows to be the largest of all garden plants and puts forth great branches, so that the birds of the air nest in its shade.”

verses.kjv: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear it.
verses.bsb: With many such parables Jesus spoke the word to them, to the extent that they could understand.

verses.kjv: But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.
verses.bsb: He did not tell them anything without using a parable. But privately He explained everything to His own disciples.

verses.kjv: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
verses.bsb: When that evening came, He said to His disciples, “Let us cross to the other side.”

verses.kjv: And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.
verses.bsb: After they had dismissed the crowd, they took Jesus with them, since He was already in the boat. And there were other boats with Him.

verses.kjv: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
verses.bsb: Soon a violent windstorm came up, and the waves were breaking over the boat, so that it was being swamped.

verses.kjv: And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?
verses.bsb: But Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on the cushion. So they woke Him and said, “Teacher, don’t You care that we are perishing?”

verses.kjv: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus got up and rebuked the wind and the sea. “Silence!” He commanded. “Be still!” And the wind died down, and it was perfectly calm.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?
verses.bsb: “Why are you so afraid?” He asked. “Do you still have no faith?”

verses.kjv: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?
verses.bsb: Overwhelmed with fear, they asked one another, “Who is this, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?”

verses.kjv: AND they came over unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.
verses.bsb: On the other side of the sea, they arrived in the region of the Gerasenes.

verses.kjv: And when he was come out of the ship, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,
verses.bsb: As soon as Jesus got out of the boat, He was met by a man with an unclean spirit, who was coming from the tombs.

verses.kjv: Who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no man could bind him, no, not with chains:
verses.bsb: This man had been living in the tombs and could no longer be restrained, even with chains.

verses.kjv: Because that he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been plucked asunder by him, and the fetters broken in pieces: neither could any man tame him.
verses.bsb: Though he was often bound with chains and shackles, he had broken the chains and shattered the shackles. Now there was no one with the strength to subdue him.

verses.kjv: And always, night and day, he was in the mountains, and in the tombs, crying, and cutting himself with stones.
verses.bsb: Night and day in the tombs and in the mountains he kept crying out and cutting himself with stones.

verses.kjv: But when he saw Jesus afar off, he ran and worshipped him,
verses.bsb: When the man saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and fell on his knees before Him.

verses.kjv: And cried with a loud voice, and said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of the most high God? I adjure thee by God, that thou torment me not.
verses.bsb: And he shouted in a loud voice, “What do You want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg You before God not to torture me!”

verses.kjv: For he said unto him, Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.
verses.bsb: For Jesus had already declared, “Come out of this man, you unclean spirit!”

verses.kjv: And he asked him, What is thy name? And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
verses.bsb: “What is your name?” Jesus asked.  “My name is Legion,” he replied, “for we are many.”

verses.kjv: And he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country.
verses.bsb: And he begged Jesus repeatedly not to send them out of that region.

verses.kjv: Now there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding.
verses.bsb: There on the nearby hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.

verses.kjv: And all the devils besought him, saying, Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.
verses.bsb: So the demons begged Jesus, “Send us to the pigs, so that we may enter them.”

verses.kjv: And forthwith Jesus gave them leave. And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea, (they were about two thousand;) and were choked in the sea.
verses.bsb: He gave them permission, and the unclean spirits came out and went into the pigs, and the herd of about two thousand rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned in the water.

verses.kjv: And they that fed the swine fled, and told it in the city, and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that was done.
verses.bsb: Those tending the pigs ran off and reported this in the town and countryside, and the people went out to see what had happened.

verses.kjv: And they come to Jesus, and see him that was possessed with the devil, and had the legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.
verses.bsb: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons sitting there, clothed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.

verses.kjv: And they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil, and also concerning the swine.
verses.bsb: Those who had seen it described what had happened to the demon-possessed man and also to the pigs.

verses.kjv: And they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts.
verses.bsb: And the people began to beg Jesus to leave their region.

verses.kjv: And when he was come into the ship, he that had been possessed with the devil prayed him that he might be with him.
verses.bsb: As He was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed by the demons begged to go with Him.

verses.kjv: Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.
verses.bsb: But Jesus would not allow him. “Go home to your own people,” He said, “and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and what mercy He has shown you.”

verses.kjv: And he departed, and began to publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done for him: and all men did marvel.
verses.bsb: So the man went away and began to proclaim throughout the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him. And everyone was amazed.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus was passed over again by ship unto the other side, much people gathered unto him: and he was nigh unto the sea.
verses.bsb: When Jesus had again crossed by boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around Him beside the sea.

verses.kjv: And, behold, there cometh one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name; and when he saw him, he fell at his feet,
verses.bsb: A synagogue leader named Jairus arrived, and seeing Jesus, he fell at His feet

verses.kjv: And besought him greatly, saying, My little daughter lieth at the point of death: I pray thee, come and lay thy hands on her, that she may be healed; and she shall live.
verses.bsb: and pleaded with Him urgently, “My little daughter is near death. Please come and place Your hands on her, so that she will be healed and live.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus went with him; and much people followed him, and thronged him.
verses.bsb: So Jesus went with him, and a large crowd followed and pressed around Him.

verses.kjv: And a certain woman, which had an issue of blood twelve years,
verses.bsb: And a woman was there who had suffered from bleeding for twelve years.

verses.kjv: And had suffered many things of many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse,
verses.bsb: She had borne much agony under the care of many physicians and had spent all she had, but to no avail. Instead, her condition had only grown worse.

verses.kjv: When she had heard of Jesus, came in the press behind, and touched his garment.
verses.bsb: When the woman heard about Jesus, she came up through the crowd behind Him and touched His cloak.

verses.kjv: For she said, If I may touch but his clothes, I shall be whole.
verses.bsb: For she kept saying, “If only I touch His garments, I will be healed.”

verses.kjv: And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.
verses.bsb: Immediately her bleeding stopped, and she sensed in her body that she was healed of her affliction.

verses.kjv: And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, Who touched my clothes?
verses.bsb: At once Jesus was aware that power had gone out from Him. Turning to the crowd, He asked, “Who touched My garments?”

verses.kjv: And his disciples said unto him, Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
verses.bsb: His disciples answered, “You can see the crowd pressing in on You, and yet You ask, ‘Who touched Me?’”

verses.kjv: And he looked round about to see her that had done this thing.
verses.bsb: But He kept looking around to see who had done this.

verses.kjv: But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
verses.bsb: Then the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him trembling in fear, and she told Him the whole truth.

verses.kjv: And he said unto her, Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague.
verses.bsb: “Daughter,” said Jesus, “your faith has healed you. Go in peace and be free of your affliction.”

verses.kjv: While he yet spake, there came from the ruler of the synagogue’s house certain which said, Thy daughter is dead: why troublest thou the Master any further?
verses.bsb: While He was still speaking, messengers from the house of Jairus arrived and said, “Your daughter is dead; why bother the Teacher anymore?”

verses.kjv: As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, Be not afraid, only believe.
verses.bsb: But Jesus overheard their conversation and said to Jairus, “Do not be afraid; just believe.”

verses.kjv: And he suffered no man to follow him, save Peter, and James, and John the brother of James.
verses.bsb: And He did not allow anyone to accompany Him except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.

verses.kjv: And he cometh to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and seeth the tumult, and them that wept and wailed greatly.
verses.bsb: When they arrived at the house of the synagogue leader, Jesus saw the commotion and the people weeping and wailing loudly.

verses.kjv: And when he was come in, he saith unto them, Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.
verses.bsb: He went inside and asked, “Why all this commotion and weeping? The child is not dead, but asleep.”

verses.kjv: And they laughed him to scorn. But when he had put them all out, he taketh the father and the mother of the damsel, and them that were with him, and entereth in where the damsel was lying.
verses.bsb: And they laughed at Him.  After He had put them all outside, He took the child’s father and mother and His own companions, and went in to see the child.

verses.kjv: And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, arise.
verses.bsb: Taking her by the hand, Jesus said, “Talitha koum!” which means, “Little girl, I say to you, get up!”

verses.kjv: And straightway the damsel arose, and walked; for she was of the age of twelve years. And they were astonished with a great astonishment.
verses.bsb: Immediately the girl got up and began to walk around. She was twelve years old, and at once they were utterly astounded.

verses.kjv: And he charged them straitly that no man should know it; and commanded that something should be given her to eat.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus gave strict orders that no one should know about this, and He told them to give her something to eat.

verses.kjv: AND he went out from thence, and came into his own country; and his disciples follow him.
verses.bsb: Jesus went on from there and came to His hometown, accompanied by His disciples.

verses.kjv: And when the sabbath day was come, he began to teach in the synagogue: and many hearing him were astonished, saying, From whence hath this man these things? and what wisdom is this which is given unto him, that even such mighty works are wrought by his hands?
verses.bsb: When the Sabbath came, He began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard Him were astonished. “Where did this man get these ideas?” they asked. “What is this wisdom He has been given? And how can He perform such miracles?

verses.kjv: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joses, and of Juda, and Simon? and are not his sisters here with us? And they were offended at him.
verses.bsb: Isn’t this the carpenter, the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? Aren’t His sisters here with us as well?” And they took offense at Him.

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “Only in his hometown, among his relatives, and in his own household is a prophet without honor.”

verses.kjv: And he could there do no mighty work, save that he laid his hands upon a few sick folk, and healed them.
verses.bsb: So He could not perform any miracles there, except to lay His hands on a few of the sick and heal them.

verses.kjv: And he marvelled because of their unbelief. And he went round about the villages, teaching.
verses.bsb: And He was amazed at their unbelief.  And He went around from village to village, teaching the people.

verses.kjv: ¶ And he called unto him the twelve, and began to send them forth by two and two; and gave them power over unclean spirits;
verses.bsb: Then Jesus called the Twelve to Him and began to send them out two by two, giving them authority over unclean spirits.

verses.kjv: And commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, save a staff only; no scrip, no bread, no money in their purse:
verses.bsb: He instructed them to take nothing but a staff for the journey—no bread, no bag, no money in their belts—

verses.kjv: But be shod with sandals; and not put on two coats.
verses.bsb: and to wear sandals, but not a second tunic.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, In what place soever ye enter into an house, there abide till ye depart from that place.
verses.bsb: And He told them, “When you enter a house, stay there until you leave that area.

verses.kjv: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them. Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
verses.bsb: If anyone will not welcome you or listen to you, shake the dust off your feet when you leave that place, as a testimony against them.”

verses.kjv: And they went out, and preached that men should repent.
verses.bsb: So they set out and preached that the people should repent.

verses.kjv: And they cast out many devils, and anointed with oil many that were sick, and healed them.
verses.bsb: They also drove out many demons and healed many of the sick, anointing them with oil.

verses.kjv: And king Herod heard of him; (for his name was spread abroad:) and he said, That John the Baptist was risen from the dead, and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
verses.bsb: Now King Herod heard about this, for Jesus’ name had become well known, and people were saying, “John the Baptist has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are at work in him.”

verses.kjv: Others said, That it is Elias. And others said, That it is a prophet, or as one of the prophets.
verses.bsb: Others were saying, “He is Elijah,” and still others, “He is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old.”

verses.kjv: But when Herod heard thereof, he said, It is John, whom I beheaded: he is risen from the dead.
verses.bsb: But when Herod heard this, he said, “John, whom I beheaded, has risen from the dead!”

verses.kjv: For Herod himself had sent forth and laid hold upon John, and bound him in prison for Herodias’ sake, his brother Philip’s wife: for he had married her.
verses.bsb: For Herod himself had ordered that John be arrested and bound and imprisoned, on account of his brother Philip’s wife Herodias, whom Herod had married.

verses.kjv: For John had said unto Herod, It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother’s wife.
verses.bsb: For John had been telling Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife!”

verses.kjv: Therefore Herodias had a quarrel against him, and would have killed him; but she could not:
verses.bsb: So Herodias held a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she had been unable,

verses.kjv: For Herod feared John, knowing that he was a just man and an holy, and observed him; and when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly.
verses.bsb: because Herod feared John and protected him, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man. When he heard John’s words, he was greatly perplexed; yet he listened to him gladly.

verses.kjv: And when a convenient day was come, that Herod on his birthday made a supper to his lords, high captains, and chief estates of Galilee;
verses.bsb: On Herod’s birthday, her opportunity arose. Herod held a banquet for his nobles and military commanders and the leading men of Galilee.

verses.kjv: And when the daughter of the said Herodias came in, and danced, and pleased Herod and them that sat with him, the king said unto the damsel, Ask of me whatsoever thou wilt, and I will give it thee.
verses.bsb: When the daughter of Herodias came and danced, she pleased Herod and his guests, and the king said to the girl, “Ask me for whatever you wish, and I will give it to you.”

verses.kjv: And he sware unto her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask of me, I will give it thee, unto the half of my kingdom.
verses.bsb: And he swore to her, “Whatever you ask of me, I will give you, up to half my kingdom!”

verses.kjv: And she went forth, and said unto her mother, What shall I ask? And she said, The head of John the Baptist.
verses.bsb: Then she went out and asked her mother, “What should I request?”  And her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist.”

verses.kjv: And she came in straightway with haste unto the king, and asked, saying, I will that thou give me by and by in a charger the head of John the Baptist.
verses.bsb: At once the girl hurried back to the king with her request: “I want you to give me the head of John the Baptist on a platter immediately.”

verses.kjv: And the king was exceeding sorry; yet for his oath’s sake, and for their sakes which sat with him, he would not reject her.
verses.bsb: The king was consumed with sorrow, but because of his oaths and his guests, he did not want to refuse her.

verses.kjv: And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
verses.bsb: So without delay, the king commanded that John’s head be brought in. He sent an executioner, who went and beheaded him in the prison.

verses.kjv: And brought his head in a charger, and gave it to the damsel: and the damsel gave it to her mother.
verses.bsb: The man brought John’s head on a platter and presented it to the girl, who gave it to her mother.

verses.kjv: And when his disciples heard of it, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
verses.bsb: When John’s disciples heard about this, they came and took his body and placed it in a tomb.

verses.kjv: And the apostles gathered themselves together unto Jesus, and told him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the apostles gathered around Jesus and brought Him news of all they had done and taught.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
verses.bsb: And He said to them, “Come with Me privately to a solitary place, and let us rest for a while.” For many people were coming and going, and they did not even have time to eat.

verses.kjv: And they departed into a desert place by ship privately.
verses.bsb: So they went away in a boat by themselves to a solitary place.

verses.kjv: And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.
verses.bsb: But many people saw them leaving and recognized them. They ran together on foot from all the towns and arrived before them.

verses.kjv: And Jesus, when he came out, saw much people, and was moved with compassion toward them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd: and he began to teach them many things.
verses.bsb: When Jesus stepped ashore and saw a large crowd, He had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. And He began to teach them many things.

verses.kjv: And when the day was now far spent, his disciples came unto him, and said, This is a desert place, and now the time is far passed:
verses.bsb: By now the hour was already late. So the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This is a desolate place, and the hour is already late.

verses.kjv: Send them away, that they may go into the country round about, and into the villages, and buy themselves bread: for they have nothing to eat.
verses.bsb: Dismiss the crowd so they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat.”

verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
verses.bsb: But Jesus told them, “You give them something to eat.”  They asked Him, “Should we go out and spend two hundred denarii to give all of them bread to eat?”

verses.kjv: He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
verses.bsb: “Go and see how many loaves you have,” He told them.  And after checking, they said, “Five—and two fish.”

verses.kjv: And he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus directed them to have the people sit in groups on the green grass.

verses.kjv: And they sat down in ranks, by hundreds, and by fifties.
verses.bsb: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.

verses.kjv: And when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes divided he among them all.
verses.bsb: Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, Jesus spoke a blessing and broke the loaves. Then He gave them to His disciples to set before the people. And He divided the two fish among them all.

verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled.
verses.bsb: They all ate and were satisfied,

verses.kjv: And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.
verses.bsb: and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces of bread and fish.

verses.kjv: And they that did eat of the loaves were about five thousand men.
verses.bsb: And there were five thousand men who had eaten the loaves.

verses.kjv: And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to go to the other side before unto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.
verses.bsb: Immediately Jesus made His disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of Him to Bethsaida, while He dismissed the crowd.

verses.kjv: And when he had sent them away, he departed into a mountain to pray.
verses.bsb: After bidding them farewell, He went up on the mountain to pray.

verses.kjv: And when even was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone on the land.
verses.bsb: When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and Jesus was alone on land.

verses.kjv: And he saw them toiling in rowing; for the wind was contrary unto them: and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea, and would have passed by them.
verses.bsb: He could see that the disciples were straining to row, because the wind was against them. About the fourth watch of the night, Jesus went out to them, walking on the sea. He intended to pass by them,

verses.kjv: But when they saw him walking upon the sea, they supposed it had been a spirit, and cried out:
verses.bsb: but when they saw Him walking on the sea, they cried out, thinking He was a ghost—

verses.kjv: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.
verses.bsb: for they all saw Him and were terrified.  But Jesus spoke up at once: “Take courage! It is I. Do not be afraid.”

verses.kjv: And he went up unto them into the ship; and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure, and wondered.
verses.bsb: Then He climbed into the boat with them, and the wind died down. And the disciples were utterly astounded,

verses.kjv: For they considered not the miracle of the loaves: for their heart was hardened.
verses.bsb: for they had not understood about the loaves, but their hearts had been hardened.

verses.kjv: And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Gennesaret, and drew to the shore.
verses.bsb: When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret and moored the boat.

verses.kjv: And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,
verses.bsb: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus

verses.kjv: And ran through that whole region round about, and began to carry about in beds those that were sick, where they heard he was.
verses.bsb: and ran through that whole region, carrying the sick on mats to wherever they heard He was.

verses.kjv: And whithersoever he entered, into villages, or cities, or country, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him were made whole.
verses.bsb: And wherever He went—villages and towns and countrysides—they laid the sick in the marketplaces and begged Him just to let them touch the fringe of His cloak. And all who touched Him were healed.

verses.kjv: THEN came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus,

verses.kjv: And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.
verses.bsb: and they saw some of His disciples eating with hands that were defiled—that is, unwashed.

verses.kjv: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they wash their hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.
verses.bsb: Now in holding to the tradition of the elders, the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat until they wash their hands ceremonially.

verses.kjv: And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.
verses.bsb: And on returning from the market, they do not eat unless they wash. And there are many other traditions for them to observe, including the washing of cups, pitchers, kettles, and couches for dining.

verses.kjv: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashen hands?
verses.bsb: So the Pharisees and scribes questioned Jesus: “Why do Your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders? Instead, they eat with defiled hands.”

verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered them, “Isaiah prophesied correctly about you hypocrites, as it is written:  ‘These people honor Me with their lips, but their hearts are far from Me.

verses.kjv: Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
verses.bsb: They worship Me in vain; they teach as doctrine the precepts of men.’

verses.kjv: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.
verses.bsb: You have disregarded the commandment of God to keep the tradition of men.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.
verses.bsb: He went on to say, “You neatly set aside the command of God to maintain your own tradition.

verses.kjv: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:
verses.bsb: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’ and ‘Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.’

verses.kjv: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.
verses.bsb: But you say that if a man says to his father or mother, ‘Whatever you would have received from me is Corban’ (that is, a gift devoted to God),

verses.kjv: And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;
verses.bsb: he is no longer permitted to do anything for his father or mother.

verses.kjv: Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.
verses.bsb: Thus you nullify the word of God by the tradition you have handed down. And you do so in many such matters.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus called the crowd to Him and said, “All of you, listen to Me and understand:

verses.kjv: There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.
verses.bsb: Nothing that enters a man from the outside can defile him; but the things that come out of a man, these are what defile him.”

verses.kjv: And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had left the crowd and gone into the house, His disciples inquired about the parable.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
verses.bsb: “Are you still so dull?” He asked. “Do you not understand? Nothing that enters a man from the outside can defile him,

verses.kjv: Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
verses.bsb: because it does not enter his heart, but it goes into the stomach and then is eliminated.” (Thus all foods are clean.)

verses.kjv: And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.
verses.bsb: He continued: “What comes out of a man, that is what defiles him.

verses.kjv: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,
verses.bsb: For from within the hearts of men come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,

verses.kjv: Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:
verses.bsb: greed, wickedness, deceit, debauchery, envy, slander, arrogance, and foolishness.

verses.kjv: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.
verses.bsb: All these evils come from within, and these are what defile a man.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, and would have no man know it: but he could not be hid.
verses.bsb: Jesus left that place and went to the region of Tyre. Not wanting anyone to know He was there, He entered a house, but was unable to escape their notice.

verses.kjv: For a certain woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:
verses.bsb: Instead, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit soon heard about Jesus, and she came and fell at His feet.

verses.kjv: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
verses.bsb: Now she was a Greek woman of Syrophoenician origin, and she kept asking Jesus to drive the demon out of her daughter.

verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.
verses.bsb: “First let the children have their fill,” He said. “For it is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to the dogs.”

verses.kjv: And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children’s crumbs.
verses.bsb: “Yes, Lord,” she replied, “even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”

verses.kjv: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told her, “Because of this answer, you may go. The demon has left your daughter.”

verses.kjv: And when she was come to her house, she found the devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.
verses.bsb: And she went home and found her child lying on the bed, and the demon was gone.

verses.kjv: ¶ And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus left the region of Tyre and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee and into the region of the Decapolis.

verses.kjv: And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his hand upon him.
verses.bsb: Some people brought to Him a man who was deaf and hardly able to speak, and they begged Jesus to place His hand on him.

verses.kjv: And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
verses.bsb: So Jesus took him aside privately, away from the crowd, and put His fingers into the man’s ears. Then He spit and touched the man’s tongue.

verses.kjv: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
verses.bsb: And looking up to heaven, He sighed deeply and said to him, “Ephphatha!” (which means, “Be opened!”).

verses.kjv: And straightway his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.
verses.bsb: Immediately the man’s ears were opened and his tongue was released, and he began to speak plainly.

verses.kjv: And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they published it;
verses.bsb: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone. But the more He ordered them, the more widely they proclaimed it.

verses.kjv: And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.
verses.bsb: The people were utterly astonished and said, “He has done all things well! He makes even the deaf hear and the mute speak!”

verses.kjv: IN those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples unto him, and saith unto them,
verses.bsb: In those days the crowd once again became very large, and they had nothing to eat. Jesus called the disciples to Him and said,

verses.kjv: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
verses.bsb: “I have compassion for this crowd, because they have already been with Me three days and have nothing to eat.

verses.kjv: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.
verses.bsb: If I send them home hungry, they will faint along the way. For some of them have come a great distance.”

verses.kjv: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?
verses.bsb: His disciples replied, “Where in this desolate place could anyone find enough bread to feed all these people?”

verses.kjv: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
verses.bsb: “How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked.  “Seven,” they replied.

verses.kjv: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people.
verses.bsb: And He instructed the crowd to sit down on the ground. Then He took the seven loaves, gave thanks and broke them, and gave them to His disciples to set before the people. And they distributed them to the crowd.

verses.kjv: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before them.
verses.bsb: They also had a few small fish, and Jesus blessed them and ordered that these be set before them as well.

verses.kjv: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets.
verses.bsb: The people ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

verses.kjv: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.
verses.bsb: And about four thousand men were present.  As soon as Jesus had dismissed the crowd,

verses.kjv: ¶ And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
verses.bsb: He got into the boat with His disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.

verses.kjv: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees came and began to argue with Jesus, testing Him by demanding from Him a sign from heaven.

verses.kjv: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
verses.bsb: Jesus sighed deeply in His spirit and said, “Why does this generation demand a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”

verses.kjv: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.
verses.bsb: And He left them, got back into the boat, and crossed to the other side.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.
verses.bsb: Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, except for one loaf they had with them in the boat.

verses.kjv: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
verses.bsb: “Watch out!” He cautioned them. “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of Herod.”

verses.kjv: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have no bread.
verses.bsb: So they began to discuss with one another the fact that they had no bread.

verses.kjv: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
verses.bsb: Aware of their conversation, Jesus asked them, “Why are you debating about having no bread? Do you still not see or understand? Do you have such hard hearts?

verses.kjv: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
verses.bsb: ‘Having eyes, do you not see? And having ears, do you not hear?’ And do you not remember?

verses.kjv: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
verses.bsb: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces did you collect?”  “Twelve,” they answered.

verses.kjv: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
verses.bsb: “And when I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces did you collect?”  “Seven,” they said.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
verses.bsb: Then He asked them, “Do you still not understand?”

verses.kjv: ¶ And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.
verses.bsb: When they arrived at Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man and begged Jesus to touch him.

verses.kjv: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.
verses.bsb: So He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village. Then He spit on the man’s eyes and placed His hands on him. “Can you see anything?” He asked.

verses.kjv: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.
verses.bsb: The man looked up and said, “I can see the people, but they look like trees walking around.”

verses.kjv: After that he put his hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus placed His hands on the man’s eyes, and when he opened them his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.

verses.kjv: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
verses.bsb: Jesus sent him home and said, “Do not go back into the village.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cæsarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus and His disciples went on to the villages around Caesarea Philippi. On the way, He questioned His disciples: “Who do people say I am?”

verses.kjv: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some say, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.
verses.bsb: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets.”

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
verses.bsb: “But what about you?” Jesus asked. “Who do you say I am?”  Peter answered, “You are the Christ.”

verses.kjv: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.
verses.bsb: And Jesus warned them not to tell anyone about Him.

verses.kjv: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and of the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
verses.bsb: Then He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and that He must be killed and after three days rise again.

verses.kjv: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.
verses.bsb: He spoke this message quite frankly, and Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him.

verses.kjv: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
verses.bsb: But Jesus, turning and looking at His disciples, rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind Me, Satan! For you do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus called the crowd to Him along with His disciples, and He told them, “If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow Me.

verses.kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel’s, the same shall save it.
verses.bsb: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake and for the gospel will save it.

verses.kjv: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
verses.bsb: What does it profit a man to gain the whole world, yet forfeit his soul?

verses.kjv: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
verses.bsb: Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?

verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
